compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: WS5 remediation complete (pending re-review): fixed the 3 CRITICAL bugs from first review — (1) gitleaks now writes SARIF to a tempfile + ABSTAINS on no parseable report (was /dev/stdout → silent 0-findings fail-OPEN); (2) _run_sarif re-attributes records to the sentinel id (was gitleaks' own ruleId, dropped by the consumer prefix filter); (3) absolute SARIF URIs relativized to repo_root for diff-scope. Added real-gitleaks end-to-end test (planted high-entropy PAT → BLOCK) + deterministic _run_sarif chain tests (re-attribute/relativize/abstain) + empty-changed-files + grounding_info sarif-backend test. run_security_detectors now defaults a None repo_root to cwd (never silently skips). Full suite: 274 passed; ruff+mypy clean. Re-review dispatched.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Re-review (subagent, adversarial): VERDICT PASS. All 3 CRITICAL bugs verified genuinely fixed — reviewer reproduced the chain against real gitleaks 8.30.1: (1) tempfile-report + abstain-on-no-parseable-SARIF (no silent fail-OPEN; clean run → [] not abstain); (2) re-attribution to sentinel id (consumer prefix filter keeps records); (3) absolute→repo-relative URI relativization (diff-scope matches). Fail-OPEN oracle invariant preserved (evidence vocab has no 'block' outcome). Tests non-vacuous on the fixed paths. Advisory MINOR #1 (scanners are a runtime dependency of the enabled gate → fail-closed BLOCK if absent) remediated with an operator note in ADR 0012. MINOR #2 (multi-SARIF-detector attribution) + the pre-existing registry.py:252 precedence are forward-looking/out-of-scope, filed mentally as non-blocking. Proceeding to commit + close.

compiled_state.description:
Wire **secrets (gitleaks) + High-Critical security (semgrep)** scanners as deterministic
ground truth feeding the hard auto-block (the LLM triages/prioritizes, never invents). The
integration shape below is settled by a derisk experiment (session_log a7e2-381c-6dc6-4d0c,
EXPERIMENT 1; tools: gitleaks 8.30.1, semgrep 1.168.0 against Engine B at
`src/rebar/grounding/`).

## Detector integration — what actually fits Engine B (experiment-grounded)

Engine B has three backends (`registry.py`: `opengrep`, `ast-grep`, `metric`); a detector is a
native matcher YAML rule + `metadata.rebar_envelope`, parsed via `yaml.safe_load`.

- **semgrep = NATIVE fit.** semgrep is the opengrep rule format; PROVEN it passes
  `metadata.rebar_envelope` through verbatim + emits SARIF 2.1.0. High-Critical security
  detectors ship as **detector YAML under `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/builtin/security_*.yaml`
  consumed by the existing `opengrep` backend** — no new backend. **Named rulesets:** a curated,
  vendored, pinned subset of `p/owasp-top-ten` + `p/cwe-top-25` (NOT a live registry pull —
  reproducible/offline). Diff-scoped (changed files only); full-repo is out of scope for v1.
- **gitleaks ≠ native fit.** PROVEN: gitleaks uses TOML config with rules compiled into the
  binary (no per-rule YAML), so it cannot be a per-rule Engine B detector. It emits SARIF 2.1.0.
  gitleaks is integrated via a generic SARIF-ingest backend (`BACKEND_SARIF`): a `_run_sarif()`
  runner that subprocess-invokes the tool (`gitleaks detect --report-format sarif`), then
  normalizes via `sarif.from_sarif(..., trust_rebar_bag=False)`. Chosen over a gitleaks-only 4th
  backend because semgrep ALSO emits SARIF — one seam serves both.

## SARIF-backend dispatch (resolves the G6 empty-registry BLOCK)
Engine B's `scan()` only invokes a backend when `applicable_by_backend[backend]` is non-empty —
which requires a registered `Detector`. gitleaks has no per-rule YAML, so WITHOUT a fix the
`sarif` backend would never dispatch. **Committed mechanism (one, not a fork):** ship a SENTINEL
detector descriptor `detectors/builtin/gitleaks.yaml` — a single `Detector` with `backend:
sarif`, `metadata.rebar_envelope`, and a tool reference (the command + report format) INSTEAD of
matcher rules (gitleaks carries its own compiled rules). Its presence puts one `Detector` in
`applicable_by_backend["sarif"]`, so the existing dispatch loop routes to `_run_sarif`, which
runs the tool once and ingests its SARIF (the sentinel's per-rule body is empty — the tool, not
the YAML, supplies the rules). No bypass code path in `scan()`; the registry-driven dispatch is
preserved. `_run_sarif`/`BACKEND_SARIF` is NEW engine code (a 4th backend runner in
`_BACKEND_RUNNERS`) + its own test.

## Fail-CLOSED lives in the CONSUMER, not the oracle (experiment-grounded)
PROVEN: Engine B is **fail-OPEN** — a missing/errored/timed-out binary returns
`abstain('no_tool'|'timeout'|'other')`, never blocks (`engine_b.py`; `docs/grounding.md`).
Forcing the oracle fail-closed would break every other consumer. So the **fail-closed decision
is made by THIS gate's verdict assembly** (the deterministic consumer step), NOT the oracle.

**Abstain→BLOCK bridge — ONE committed design (resolves the G6 fork BLOCK):** the bridge is a
deterministic VERDICT-ASSEMBLY forced-BLOCK short-circuit, NOT a synthetic Pass-1 finding. (The
synthetic-finding path is rejected: a finding with no real Pass-2 verification hits
`pass3_decide(None) → INDETERMINATE`, which can NOT block.) Concretely: `produce_code_review_verdict`
(WS4) reads the oracle evidence for the secrets/High-Critical-security detectors; if any returns
`abstain` (tool unavailable/errored/timed out) OR a real positive finding, the verdict is forced
to `BLOCK` with a `coverage-gap` annotation on the vote (distinguishing a fail-closed abstain
from a real-finding block), AFTER and INDEPENDENT of the normal Pass-3 decision over the LLM
findings. This is deterministic and never fights `pass3_decide`. WS5 owns this forced-BLOCK
consumer step + sets `blocking_enabled=True` for exactly the two detector criteria via WS2's
`threshold_for` resolver (the WS2→WS5 handoff).

## Vendored-rule maintenance (resolves the T5e staleness BLOCK)
The vendored `security_*.yaml` rules are pinned for reproducibility but must not silently rot.
Mechanism: a `make vendor-security-rules` target re-pulls + re-pins the curated subset from the
upstream registry at a recorded version, and a CI freshness check warns when the vendored
pinned version is older than a stated cadence (e.g. quarterly). The cadence + the refresh
command are documented in the WS5 ADR + an ops note. (v1 ships the pinned snapshot; the
refresh is a documented periodic PR, not an automated live pull.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] semgrep High-Critical security detectors ship as vendored, pinned detector YAML under `src/rebar/grounding/detectors/builtin/security_*.yaml` (named subset of p/owasp-top-ten + p/cwe-top-25 documented), consumed by the existing `opengrep` backend; diff-scoped.
- [ ] gitleaks integrated via the `BACKEND_SARIF` runner (`_run_sarif` → `sarif.from_sarif(trust_rebar_bag=False)`), routed by a SENTINEL `detectors/builtin/gitleaks.yaml` descriptor (backend: sarif, no per-rule body) so the registry dispatch reaches it WITHOUT bypassing `scan()`; a test asserts a planted secret surfaces as a normalized evidence finding.
- [ ] Secrets / High-Critical criteria map to a hard BLOCK; everything else stays advisory at the high starting thresholds (`blocking_enabled=True` set for exactly those two criteria via WS2's resolver — explicit WS2→WS5 handoff).
- [ ] FAIL-CLOSED in the CONSUMER (verdict assembly): RED-fixture test asserts an unavailable/errored secrets/security detector → the verdict is forced to BLOCK + a coverage-gap annotation (distinct from a real-finding block), NEVER a silent pass; the oracle itself stays fail-OPEN (a separate test pins the oracle still returns abstain, not block).
- [ ] The abstain→BLOCK bridge is the deterministic verdict-assembly forced-BLOCK short-circuit (NOT a synthetic Pass-1 finding — `pass3_decide(None)→INDETERMINATE` cannot block); a test pins the exact interface (oracle abstain on a detector criterion → verdict=BLOCK).
- [ ] HAPPY-PATH test: a detector returning real findings surfaces them as ground-truth blocking findings.
- [ ] "Diff-scoped" is a POST-FILTER of Engine B findings to the assembled `changed_files` set (Engine B `scan()` takes only `repo_root`); a test asserts an unchanged-file finding is filtered out.
- [ ] `_run_sarif`/`BACKEND_SARIF` is NEW engine code (a 4th backend runner in `_BACKEND_RUNNERS`) with its own test.
- [ ] An ADR records: the SARIF-ingest-backend choice (vs a gitleaks-only 4th backend), the SENTINEL-detector dispatch mechanism, the consumer-side (verdict-assembly) fail-closed boundary, and the vendored-rule refresh cadence/command.
- [ ] A `make vendor-security-rules` target + a CI freshness check exist; the refresh cadence is documented.

## Plan-review remediation (Round-12, 2026-06-30)
- BLOCK (G6 dispatch): committed to the SENTINEL gitleaks detector descriptor so the registry-driven `scan()` dispatch reaches `_run_sarif` with zero per-rule YAML — no `scan()` bypass.
- BLOCK (G6 abstain→BLOCK fork): committed to the deterministic verdict-assembly forced-BLOCK bridge; removed the synthetic-Pass-1-finding alternative (defeated by `pass3_decide(None)→INDETERMINATE`).
- BLOCK (T5e staleness): added the `make vendor-security-rules` refresh + CI freshness check + documented cadence.
compiled_state.title: WS5: Secrets/security detectors (gitleaks/semgrep) behind the grounding-oracle envelope — fail-closed + RED-fixture test
